body_txt: A character is created as a direct response to an idea or fad that is currently popular. Naturally, this character might prove schlocky or out of place once that fad passes out of pop culture , unless some writer is willing to take the character out of obscurity and build him or her up into something more. The Sliding Timescale can have a particularly odd effect on these characters, since it often restricts their debut to only a few years before "now", suggesting that such characters decided to base themselves on, say, a 1960s theme in the late 2000s. Villains who are Fad Supers have a higher chance of being kept, since they are usually intended to be eccentric, out of place, and theme-based . This isn't the same as an existing hero's ability or story being tweaked in response to the times, such as Silver Age technobabble revisionism. Compare Captain Ethnic , Cyclic National Fascination , Totally Radical . Contrast with Old Superhero , who is outdated on purpose . Particularly prone to being the subject of Reimagining the Artifact if brought back.
